description:
You are a backup-integrity engineer who has just received a weekly backup archive from the operations team.  
Your job is to perform two checks in parallel:

1. Integrity check → calculate the archive’s SHA-256 hash and confirm it matches the expected hash value supplied by operations.  
2. Basic security scan → unpack the archive and look for signs of malicious code (in this simplified scenario: any line that contains the literal text “eval(” in any extracted file).

When you finish, create a single, human-readable log file whose exact format is given below.  
All work must be done inside your home directory without elevated privileges.

Directory / file layout that already exists when you begin:
• /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.tar.gz ← the archive to be checked  
• /home/user/backups/expected_sha256.txt ← contains **one** 64-character SHA-256 hash followed by two spaces and the file name “weekly_backup.tar.gz”

You must create the following working directory:
• /home/user/backup_audit/

Inside /home/user/backup_audit/ you must end up with:
• extracted/                 ← the directory that holds the decompressed backup contents  
• verification_report.log    ← the mandatory final report (specification below)

Specification of verification_report.log
────────────────────────────────────────
The report is a plain-text file encoded in UTF-8.  
Its content must be **exactly** eight lines (no extra blank lines, no trailing spaces).  
The automated tests will perform a strict byte-for-byte comparison, so reproduce the format precisely:

Line 1: BACKUP_VERIFICATION_REPORT  
Line 2: Timestamp: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ              ← UTC ISO-8601 timestamp generated at runtime  
Line 3: Backup_File: /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.tar.gz  
Line 4: SHA256_Expected: <hash from expected_sha256.txt>  
Line 5: SHA256_Actual: <hash you calculated>  
Line 6: Integrity: PASS  or  FAIL                    ← PASS only if the two hashes are identical  
Line 7: Virus_Scan: CLEAN  or  INFECTED              ← CLEAN only if zero “eval(” occurrences were found  
Line 8: Files_Checked: <N>                           ← integer count of regular files extracted

Additional functional requirements
1. The archive must be extracted into /home/user/backup_audit/extracted/  
2. The SHA-256 hash must be calculated directly from /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.tar.gz (do not hash a copy).  
3. The scan must recurse through every regular file inside extracted/; count every literal substring “eval(” (case-sensitive).  
4. All commands and any intermediate temporary output should stay inside the backup_audit directory; only verification_report.log will be graded.  
5. After writing the report, verify its eight-line structure yourself by printing it to the console.

Hints (not commands)
• Typical tools: sha256sum, tar, grep, wc, date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ.  
• You can perform the checksum computation and the extraction/scan at the same time by backgrounding one of the tasks.  
• Ensure the timestamp is in UTC and ends with “Z”.  

Your work is finished when /home/user/backup_audit/verification_report.log exists and conforms exactly to the format above.
truth:
Pre-existing file contents
1. /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.tar.gz  
   The tarball contains one file:
   └── hello.txt         (contents: “Hello backup\n”)

2. /home/user/backups/expected_sha256.txt  
   87b86e47386871efdbdeffa03b1b4300243a12e3b7004302fa9ead1d231ccd24  weekly_backup.tar.gz

Expected values the tests will look for
• Correct SHA-256 of /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.tar.gz is  
  87b86e47386871efdbdeffa03b1b4300243a12e3b7004302fa9ead1d231ccd24
• Number of regular files after extraction: 1  
• Number of “eval(” occurrences: 0 → Virus_Scan must be set to CLEAN  
• Therefore “Integrity” must be PASS (hashes identical).

Verification report example with placeholders
BACKUP_VERIFICATION_REPORT
Timestamp: 2025-01-19T17:42:03Z
Backup_File: /home/user/backups/weekly_backup.tar.gz
SHA256_Expected: 87b86e47386871efdbdeffa03b1b4300243a12e3b7004302fa9ead1d231ccd24
SHA256_Actual: 87b86e47386871efdbdeffa03b1b4300243a12e3b7004302fa9ead1d231ccd24
Integrity: PASS
Virus_Scan: CLEAN
Files_Checked: 1
(The automated grader will ignore the actual timestamp but will check the other seven lines verbatim.)
name: task_000000_f68f63a5
